methodology: Articles sourced from Virginia-specific RSS feeds and Google News RSS, filtered for AI/technology/workforce relevance, then assessed by Claude against the scenario framework. Each article is tagged with the region it most affects and the scenario it signals toward.

signals.title: New partnership between VCU and AI Ready RVA aims to boost AI education, innovation and community impact - VCU News
signals.signal_reading: VCU's partnership with AI Ready RVA represents institutional commitment to distributed AI literacy beyond elite tech workers, potentially enabling peer-to-peer learning networks that could spread deep capability across Richmond's diverse communities.

signals.title: EPA grant backs community-driven workforce training in Southwest Virginia - Virginia Tech News
signals.signal_reading: EPA-funded community-driven workforce training in Southwest Virginia signals grassroots capacity-building in a historically underserved region, testing whether distributed deep learning can take root outside traditional tech corridors through local institutional anchors.

signals.title: AI pressure grows on Richmond's young workforce - Axios
signals.signal_reading: Growing AI pressure on Richmond's young workforce suggests deepening capability requirements concentrating in metro hubs, potentially widening the gap between those with access to advanced training and rural workers facing shallow platform-mediated adoption.

signals.title: Doug Hoven on How AI Is Changing the Way Homes Are Bought and Sold in Hampton Roads - The Ritz Herald
signals.signal_reading: AI integration in Hampton Roads real estate signals platform-delivered tools enabling broad but shallow adoption in traditional industries—practitioners use AI without building underlying capability, fitting the distributed-but-shallow pattern.

signals.title: Q&A: Who’s responsible when AI makes mistakes? - UVA Today
signals.signal_reading: UVA's focus on AI accountability and ethics research reinforces Virginia's concentration of deep technical and policy expertise in flagship institutions, but raises questions about whether this knowledge diffuses beyond academic centers to inform distributed practice.
